commands.repair_reason: This external PR has a useful remaining fix, but the merge conflict and missing contributor real-behavior proof require human/contributor handling rather than a repair-lane job.; Cleared: The diff changes in-process routing/policy selection, tests, and changelog text without adding dependencies, workflows, downloads, permissions, or secrets-handling changes. (sha=bd50357aff30788032f4e54d17be741a7ea27274)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads explicit agent identity into message-tool construction/routing, switches effective tool-policy session fallback to a config-aware resolver, and adds focused regression tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main still lets resolveEffectiveToolPolicy derive opaque session-key agent ids through resolveAgentIdFromSessionKey, which falls back to main instead of the configured default agent. I did not run a live embedded send in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This external PR has a useful remaining fix, but the merge conflict and missing contributor real-behavior proof require human/contributor handling rather than a repair-lane job.

target.title: [AI-assisted] fix(message): honor explicit agent routing
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the message tool derives `agentId` from `sessionKey` even when the embedded run already knows the active agent id explicitly.
- Why it matters: tool-driven sends in shared-session or delegated flows can be attributed to the wrong agent when session-key inference does not match the active embedded agent.
- What changed: explicit `agentId` now threads through OpenClaw tool construction into the message tool, and the message tool prefers that explicit value over session-derived fallback routing.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not change sandbox skill path formatting or embedded runner tool-result payload preservation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: none recorded

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Tool-driven sends now honor the active embedded agent id when one is already known, instead of always inferring routing from the session key.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: embedded Pi agent tool execution
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): message tool send path
- Relevant config (redacted): message tool invoked with both `agentId` and `agentSessionKey`

### Steps

1. Create a message tool with `agentId` set to one agent and `agentSessionKey` pointing to another.
2. Execute a `send` action.
3. Inspect the routed call to `runMessageAction`.

### Expected

- The routed call uses the explicit `agentId`.

### Actual

- Before this change, routing always preferred the `sessionKey`-derived agent id when a session key was present.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Ran `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`.
  - Confirmed explicit `agentId` wins over session-derived routing in the message tool.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Existing session-key-derived routing still works when no explicit `agentId` is provided.
  - Existing message-tool coverage still passes on latest main.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - End-to-end channel delivery against a live gateway/channel account.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - Revert this PR and fall back to session-key-derived routing only.
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts`
  - `src/agents/pi-tools.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - Tool-driven sends being attributed to the wrong agent.
  - Regressions in existing message-tool routing behavior.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  - An incorrectly threaded explicit `agentId` could override a correct session-derived route.
  - Mitigation:
    - The override is only used when callers pass `agentId`; otherwise existing fallback behavior remains unchanged.

## AI Assistance Disclosure

- This PR was prepared with AI assistance.
- I reviewed the changed code paths and understand the behavior being changed.
- Testing level: lightly tested with focused regression coverage.
- Verified with:
  - `pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- Session logs / prompts: available on request from local handoff/session notes.
target.author: slime-turing
